zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William le Boteler於大約1240年出生在英國史洛普爾維姆時，他的父親Ralph le Boteler of Oversley21歲，母親Matilda Pantulf15歲。他於1261年10月2日與Princess Angharad Verch Gruffudd在英國史洛普爾維姆結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1283年12月11日在他的家鄉享年44歲，埋葬在英國史洛普爾維姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William le Boteler于大约1240年出生在英国史洛普爾維姆时，他的父亲Ralph le Boteler of Oversley21岁，母亲Matilda Pantulf15岁。他于1261年10月2日与Princess Angharad Verch Gruffudd在英国史洛普爾維姆结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1283年12月11日在他的家乡享年44岁，埋葬在英国史洛普爾維姆。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Boteler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker of leather bottles, from an agent derivative of Middle English botel, Old French bouteille ‘bottle’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William le Boteler was born about 1240, in Wem, Shropshire, England, his father, Ralph le Boteler of Oversley, was 21 and his mother, Matilda Pantulf, was 15. He married Princess Angharad Verch Gruffudd on 2 October 1261, in Wem, Shropshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 11 December 1283, in his hometown, at the age of 44, and was buried in Wem, Shropshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William le Boteler est né environ 1240, à Wem, Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Ralph le Boteler of Oversley, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Matilda Pantulf, avait 15 ans. Il a épousé Princess Angharad Verch Gruffudd le 2 octobre 1261, à Wem, Shropshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 11 décembre 1283, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterré à Wem, Shropshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William le Boteler um 1240 in Wem, Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Ralph le Boteler of Oversley 21 und seine Mutter Matilda Pantulf 15. Er hat am 2. Oktober 1261 in Wem, Shropshire, England Princess Angharad Verch Gruffudd geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 11. Dezember 1283 im Alter von 44 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Wem, Shropshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William le Boteler è nato il circa 1240 nel Wem, Shropshire, England, il suo padre, Ralph le Boteler of Oversley, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Matilda Pantulf, aveva 15 anni. Lui sposato Princess Angharad Verch Gruffudd il 2 ottobre 1261, nel Wem, Shropshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 11 dicembre 1283, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 44 anni, e fu sepolto a Wem, Shropshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William le Botelerが約1240年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアウエムで生まれた時，父Ralph le Boteler of Oversleyは21歳，母Matilda Pantulfは15歳であった。1261年10月2日にイギリスシュロプシュアイアウエムでPrincess Angharad Verch Gruffuddと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1283年12月11日に故郷で44歳で死去し，イギリスシュロプシュアイアウエムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William le Boteler(이)가 대략 1240년 영국 슈럽실르 베에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ralph le Boteler of Oversley(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda Pantulf(은)는 15세였다. 그는 1261년 10월 2일 영국 슈럽실르 베에서 Princess Angharad Verch Gruffudd(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1283년 12월 11일 그의 고향에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 슈럽실르 베에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William le Boteler nasceu aproximadamente 1240, em Wem, Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Ralph le Boteler of Oversley, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Matilda Pantulf, tinha 15 anos. Ele casou-se com Princess Angharad Verch Gruffudd em 2 de outubro de 1261, em Wem, Shropshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 11 de dezembro de 1283, em sua cidade natal, com 44 anos, e foi sepultado em Wem, Shropshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William le Boteler родился около 1240 г. в местности вем, шропшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Ralph le Boteler of Oversley было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Matilda Pantulf, 15. Он женился на женщине по имени Princess Angharad Verch Gruffudd 2 октября 1261 г. в местности вем, шропшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 11 декабря 1283 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 44 лет и был похоронен в местности вем, шропшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William le Boteler nació aproximadamente 1240, en Wem, Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Ralph le Boteler of Oversley, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Matilda Pantulf, tenía 15 años. Se casó con Princess Angharad Verch Gruffudd el 2 de octubre de 1261, en Wem, Shropshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 11 de diciembre de 1283, en su pueblo, a la edad de 44 años, y fue sepultado en Wem, Shropshire, Inglaterra.
